title: pinguicula balcanica
intro: Pinguicula balcanica, also known as the Balkan butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species native to Europe, specifically in the Balkan Peninsula. It is a type of sundew that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting small insects. Pinguicula balcanica has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been reported to aid in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system.
